def test_knapsack_exact_capacity_fill():
    # Test case where optimal solution exactly fills capacity
    # capacity=3, items=[(3, 10), (1, 5)]
    # Optimal: take item with weight=3, value=10 (exactly fills capacity)
    # Buggy code would skip this because weight < j is False when weight=3 and j=3
    # Fixed code should use weight <= j and correctly include this item
    capacity = 3
    items = [(3, 10), (1, 5)]
    expected = 10  # Take the first item (weight=3, value=10) exactly fills capacity
    
    from source import knapsack
    result = knapsack(capacity, items)
    
    assert result == expected, f"Expected {expected}, got {result}. Bug: items that exactly fill capacity are excluded."
